topStories.title: $100 crude & 95 rupee: Why Arvind Kothari is still buying these 5 emerging themes despite the war
topStories.summary: Despite economic headwinds, Niveshaay's Arvind Kothari sees opportunity in market dips, doubling down on high-conviction themes like electrification and defence. He believes these sectors possess "war-proof" structural moats, accelerating growth due to macro shifts. Investors should brace for second-order effects but focus on long-term structural growth stories.

topStories.title: Explained! Sebi proposes sweeping changes to buyback framework. 7 things to know
topStories.summary: India's securities regulator, SEBI, is proposing major shifts in company share buybacks. Open market buybacks are set to return, and the mandatory involvement of merchant bankers may be reduced. Safeguards for promoter shareholding and minimum public shareholding are also being tightened. These changes aim to simplify business operations while enhancing investor protection.

topStories.title: Buy capital goods on dips, stay cautious on IT and consumption: Sudip Bandyopadhyay
topStories.summary: Market expert Sudip Bandyopadhyay outlines investment strategies across key sectors. He sees opportunities in State Bank of India and a generational re-rating for the pharma sector. Capital goods also present long-term buying windows. Consumption requires caution for the next two quarters. The IT sector remains in a wait-and-watch zone. Investors can build long-term portfolios by picking quality stocks.

topStories.title: Sebi sets 30-day delay for use of stock price data in educational content; effective from July 1
topStories.summary: Sebi has introduced a new rule for market data. Stock exchanges and other institutions must now delay educational price data by 30 days. This change aims to prevent misuse and confusion. The new rule starts from July 1, 2026. NISM, Sebi's training arm, gets an exception for its simulation lab. This move clarifies the difference between education and advice.

Investors should brace for second-order effects but focus on long-term structural growth stories.

business.title: Explained! Sebi proposes sweeping changes to buyback framework. 7 things to know
business.summary: India's securities regulator, SEBI, is proposing major shifts in company share buybacks. Open market buybacks are set to return, and the mandatory involvement of merchant bankers may be reduced. Safeguards for promoter shareholding and minimum public shareholding are also being tightened. These changes aim to simplify business operations while enhancing investor protection.

business.title: Sebi sets 30-day delay for use of stock price data in educational content; effective from July 1
business.summary: Sebi has introduced a new rule for market data. Stock exchanges and other institutions must now delay educational price data by 30 days. This change aims to prevent misuse and confusion. The new rule starts from July 1, 2026. NISM, Sebi's training arm, gets an exception for its simulation lab. This move clarifies the difference between education and advice.
